headlineSummary: Google Gemini is the most positive signal on a single strong praise comment, while Anthropic, OpenAI, and Microsoft Copilot cluster in a close negative tie, with Anthropic drawing both criticism and one compliment [E1][E2][E3][E4].

ranking.summary: Anthropic leans negative, but the evidence is mixed: one comment praises Opus 4.5, while two others criticize its coding output and trustworthiness [E1][E2][E3].
OpenAI is negative on limited evidence, with one comment saying GPT/Codex outputs still need major rewriting and have fundamental architectural issues [E1].
Microsoft Copilot is negative on limited evidence, with one comment tying it to creepy business practices in a Windows discussion [E4].
Google Gemini is positive on limited evidence, with one comment calling Gemini 3.0 Flash absurdly good and better than the prior baseline for the author’s work [E2].

evidence.excerpt: I've tried this consistently since GPT 3.5 came out, with every single SOTA model up to GPT 5.1 Codex Max and Opus 4.5. Every single time, I get something that works, yes, but then when I start self-reviewing the code, preparing to submit it to coworkers, I end up rewriting about 70% of the thing. So many important details are subpar about the AI solution, and many times fundamental architectural issues cripple any attempt at prompting my way out of it

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names GPT/Codex models and says their coding output is subpar enough that the author rewrites most of it.
The criticism explicitly includes Opus 4.5 among the models that require heavy rewriting and have architectural issues.

evidence.excerpt: In the frontier size bracket we have models like Opus 4.5 that are significantly better at writing code and using tools independently. In the mid tier Gemini 3.0 flash is absurdly good and is crushing the previous baseline for some of my (visual) data extraction projects.

annotations.rationale: Opus 4.5 is explicitly praised as significantly better at coding and tool use.
Gemini 3.0 flash is explicitly described as absurdly good and outperforming prior baselines on the author's projects.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic showed their true colors with their sloppy switch to using Claude Code for training data. They can absolutely do what they want but they have completely destroyed any reason for me to consider them fundamentally better than their competitors.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Anthropic and strongly condemns its behavior, saying it destroyed trust and differentiation.

evidence.excerpt: I don’t like windows due to their creepy business practices and negative design patterns in their OS so I’m very bias against it. Forcing copilot is just the latest in their creepy practices…

annotations.rationale: In a Windows 11 discussion, 'copilot' clearly refers to Microsoft's Copilot and is framed as part of creepy business practices.
